visual_features.overall_style: Clean, corporate, information-focused layout with a modern but conservative feel. Heavy use of white background, a strong aviation-blue brand color, and minimal decorative elements beyond the logo. Typography and spacing indicate a practical, documentation-style page.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven primarily by font size, weight, and capitalization. The main heading 'PRIVACY POLICY' is extremely large, bold, and all caps in dark blue, making it the clear top-level focal point. The section title '1. An overview of data protection' is the second level: large, bold, sentence case. Subheadings like 'General information' and 'Data recording on this website' are bold and slightly smaller but still prominent. Body text is smaller, regular-weight, and in a softer dark tone, clearly subordinate to headings. Navigation items are medium size and weight, visually secondary to the page title but clearly visible at the top.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: The design uses generous top and bottom padding around major headings and between the header bar and the content. Left and right margins for the text block keep content from touching screen edges, creating a central reading column. Line spacing in body copy is comfortable, aiding legibility. Whitespace around navigation links and the logo in the header is moderate, giving clear separation without feeling sparse.
unique_elements: Stylized dual-bird/wing logo in two colors (dark blue and yellow) centered or near-center in the header, strongly tying to aviation.
Large, bold all-caps legal page title that dominates the initial viewport.
Language selector rendered as small country flags on the top right of the header.
Subtle header bar drop shadow separating navigation from content.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit single-column content layout likely based on a 12-column responsive grid with a centered max-width container (approx. 1100–1200px). Header navigation appears full-width with inner content aligned to the same central container.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Standard top-fixed (or top-static) horizontal header with navigation, followed by a centered single-column content layout. Documentation-like page with stacked typographic sections.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: From top to bottom: header navigation bar (logo centered/top-middle, nav links left/right, language flags on the far right), then a large hero-style legal page title, followed by numbered section heading, subsections with subheadings, and body text paragraphs. Content appears as a linear series of text sections without visual cards.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Overall left-aligned content column. Navigation links are horizontally aligned along the top, spaced evenly. Text headings and paragraphs are left-aligned, creating a clear reading line. Logo is centered in the header by visual balance between left and right nav clusters.
responsive_hints: Central content column with generous side margins indicates the main text area will flex but remain constrained on large screens.
Horizontal nav with widely spaced items suggests that on smaller screens the nav might collapse into a stacked or hamburger menu (not visible but typical).
Font sizes and line lengths look tuned for desktop but would scale down cleanly on tablet/mobile with the same single-column pattern.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow with a single full-width content column. Header bar at the top spans the viewport and contains navigation and language selector. Below, the hero-like 'PRIVACY POLICY' heading occupies a large block; directly beneath, a numbered section heading introduces the first section. Subheadings like 'General information' and 'Data recording on this website' are stacked with body paragraphs in between. No alternating columns or sidebars are visible; the entire page reads as a continuous left-aligned document.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Header bar occupies a relatively shallow band at the top (~10–15% of initial viewport height). The main title 'PRIVACY POLICY' consumes a large vertical space immediately below, likely ~20–25% of the initial viewport, emphasizing its importance. Subsequent section headings and body copy fill the remaining area in a generous reading column. Side margins reserve roughly 10–15% of total width on each side (depending on screen), making the text column narrower than full width for comfortable reading.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Low-to-medium density. The layout is not sparse, but text is clearly separated with comfortable spacing between headings and paragraphs. It reads like an editorial/legal document rather than a dashboard: fewer UI elements, more text, with ample breathing room so content is scannable and not overwhelming.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Primary vertical flow. The eye starts at the top-left with 'Home' and the navigation, quickly anchors on the central logo, then moves to the large 'PRIVACY POLICY' heading. From there, the hierarchy guides the eye down through the numbered section heading, then through subheadings and body text. There is minimal lateral complexity; the design emphasizes straightforward downward reading.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit buttons are visible in the provided view; navigation items appear as plain text links. For consistency with this style, primary buttons would likely be solid rectangles with small-radius rounded corners (e.g., 3–4px), filled with the primary aviation blue (#00558C), white text, and no heavy shadows (flat or subtle 1–2px soft shadow). Hover state would invert or slightly darken the blue and perhaps underline text. Padding would be moderate (e.g., 10–12px vertical, 18–24px horizontal).
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Likely implemented as <button> elements or <a> links styled as buttons (e.g., <a class="btn btn-primary">), possibly wrapped in a flex container for alignment. Icon + text buttons could use a <span> or <i> element before the label within the button.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No cards are visible in this legal-text view. Based on the overall flat, corporate styling, cards elsewhere on the site would likely have a white background, a thin light-gray border (#E0E4EA) or very subtle shadow (0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)), with 12–24px internal padding and slightly rounded corners (3–4px). Headings would appear at the top, followed by smaller descriptive text or list items.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: A typical card structure would be a <div class="card"> container with child elements for title (<h3> or <h4>), optional meta information, and body content. If used for pilot information, there might be an icon or small image at the top, followed by text and perhaps CTA links at the bottom.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No form fields are visible in the screenshot. Aligned with the clean aesthetic, form inputs would likely be full-width text fields with light borders (#CCD3DE), 3–4px rounded corners, and ample vertical padding (8–10px). Labels would likely sit above the fields, left-aligned, using the same dark blue or dark neutral text. Error states might change the border to a red semantic color with a small error message below in red. Overall density would be medium, with 12–16px vertical spacing between fields.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected structure is stacked labels with full-width inputs within a <form>, using <label for="..."> above <input> or <textarea>. Related fields may be grouped in <fieldset> or wrapper <div> elements, arranged in a single column on mobile and possibly two columns on desktop when space allows.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Horizontal top navigation bar on a white background, spanning full width. Nav items ('Home', 'Pilot Information 2025', 'Service', 'Contact us') are rendered as plain text links in the primary dark blue, with generous horizontal spacing and no visible borders. The logo sits above or between nav items and visually anchors the bar. Language selector uses small rectangular flag icons on the right. Active state is not visible, but likely uses either color change (darker blue) or underline. A subtle grey drop shadow under the bar separates it from content, hinting at a slightly elevated header.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Within the visible content, list-like structure is implied via numbered section heading ('1. An overview of data protection') but no actual bullet or numbered HTML lists are visible. When used, lists would likely be simple text lists with standard bullets or numbers, aligned to the left margin of the text column, with 4–8px vertical spacing between items and no heavy dividing lines—separation achieved via whitespace and typography.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Lists are likely standard semantic <ul> or <ol> elements with <li> children. For more complex structured content (e.g., pilot info or procedures), they may use flexbox-based 'media object' patterns (small icon or badge on the left, text content on the right) within each <li> but this is not visible in the current screenshot and is inferred.

heading.visual_style: Bold, straightforward sans-serif with fairly wide characters and a strong corporate presence; large headings are all-caps and tightly spaced for impact, resembling a geometric or grotesque sans.

body.visual_style: Clean, neutral sans-serif with good legibility at smaller sizes, similar to humanist or neo-grotesque faces used in corporate documents.

spacing_system.component_padding: Horizontal and vertical padding inside structural components (header, potential cards, large headings blocks) appears to follow 16–24px increments (e.g., 16px vertical for smaller blocks, 24–32px around the main title).
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (e.g., between the main title, numbered section heading, and subsequent subsections) appears to be in the 24–40px range, roughly 3–5× the base unit.

interaction_hints: Navigation links likely change color or show an underline on hover, consistent with standard web conventions and the clean text-link styling.
The header’s subtle shadow suggests it may be sticky on scroll, remaining fixed at the top while content moves underneath, reinforcing persistent navigation.
Language flag icons are likely clickable with hover outline or slight opacity/scale change for feedback.
Buttons (where present on other pages) would benefit from smooth 150–200ms color and background transitions (e.g., background-color and box-shadow easing) to match the restrained, professional tone.
Section anchors or in-page links (for long legal content) could highlight via a slight heading color shift or scroll animation to maintain orientation in dense text.
